content.title:
Budget
Policy
*Statement* 2026
content.sub: How Kenya's budget roadmap works — and exactly where you fit in.
content.promise: In the next 10 minutes, you'll understand how the government plans public money. No jargon. No confusion. Just the facts, in your language.

content.question:
Forget the complicated name.
The BPS is the government answering *3 questions* out loud:

bullets.text: Where are we now? How's the economy actually doing?
Where do we want to go? What are our big goals for the year?
How much will we need? And how will we get the money?

content.badge: 🇰🇪 Kenya Context: BPS is the parent of every County Fiscal Strategy Paper (CFSP). Whatever BPS prioritises nationally — your county must align with it when planning local markets, health centres, and roads.

content.question:
The BPS is the *roadmap*.
The April Budget is the actual *trip.*

bullets.text: Tabled in Parliament by February 15th every year
Think of it as the government's term paper before the final exam (the April budget)
Updates every year based on new economic data — like your maps app recalculating
Also sets budget ceilings for ministries so departments can't over-budget beyond what's available

content.headline:
The 5 Core Chapters
of every BPS
BPS 2026 — The Numbers
content.debtNote: That KES 1.2T debt interest? It's money that *can't go* to roads, teachers, or healthcare. It's already spoken for before Budget Day.

content.headline:
The 5 Pillars — what the
government is betting on
content.sub: Each pillar has actual money attached. Find your tab.

pillars.title: Agriculture
pillars.desc: Irrigation, crop diversification, improved agricultural value chain

pillars.title: MSMEs (Small Business)
pillars.desc: Hustler Fund expansion, NYOTA fund, business hubs in all 47 counties

pillars.title: Healthcare
pillars.desc: Target: 35 million Kenyans covered by Universal Health Coverage + SHA

pillars.title: Housing
pillars.desc: Jobs in construction + affordable loans for homeownership

pillars.title: Digital & Creative
pillars.desc: Fibre internet + backing film, music, fashion, and the creative economy

content.question: Behind every pillar are the *systems* that make them work:

bullets.text: More roads, railways, increased power connectivity, gas exploration
National Infrastructure Fund — pooled money for big projects
Sovereign Wealth Fund — savings from natural resources

content.badge: These are the government's five open tabs. If you're a farmer, a small business owner, an artist — this is where your opportunities live. Know which tab is yours.

content.headline: ⚡ What Could Go Wrong?
content.sub: This is mature budgeting. The government is literally listing what could break.

risks.title: Climate shocks
risks.text: Drought or floods cost unplanned emergency money

risks.title: Revenue gaps
risks.text: If businesses struggle, tax collection drops

risks.title: Debt pressure
risks.text: If the shilling weakens, foreign debt gets more expensive

risks.title: Devolution drama
risks.text: Counties' unpaid bills continue to pile up

content.quote: When you know the risks, you can spot them early and ask the right questions before crisis hits.

content.question: The BPS is best described as:

options.text: The final, detailed list of every government project
The government's strategic roadmap setting priorities and spending limits

feedback.correct: ✓ Exactly! The BPS is the roadmap — priorities and ceilings — not the detailed project list. That comes later in the Estimates.
feedback.wrong: Think roadmap, not project list. The BPS sets direction and spending limits. The detailed projects come in the Estimates of Revenue & Expenditure.

content.question: In FY 2026/27, the government projects spending of KES 4.74T but revenue of only KES 3.59T. The KES 1.15T gap is called:

feedback.correct: ✓ Correct. When spending exceeds revenue, that's a fiscal deficit. Kenya bridges it through borrowing — which is why the KES 1.2T debt interest figure matters so much.
feedback.wrong: When a government spends more than it earns, that gap is a fiscal deficit — not a surplus (that's when you earn more), and not an emergency fund (that's a reserve).

content.title:
You finished
*Module 001.*
content.sub: Budget Policy Statement 2026. You now know what the government is planning, what it's betting on, and what could go wrong.

actions.title: Track the numbers
actions.sub: Check if projects match BPS priorities
